questions.question_text: You are in a hurry and driving at 70 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 70 km/h.
answers.2: I increase my speed to approximately 80 km/h.
answers.3: I reduce my speed to a maximum of 60 km/h.
answers.4: I reduce my speed to a maximum of 50 km/h.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 40 km/h, the same as the oncoming traffic. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 40 km/h.
answers.2: I pull over as far as possible to the right of the roadway.
answers.3: I apply my service brake.
answers.4: I give a flashing light signal with my high-beam headlights or a short horn signal.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 30 to 40 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead on the road. How do you proceed?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed between 30 and 40 km/h.
answers.2: I give a signal with the horn or a flashing light signal with my high-beam headlights.
answers.3: I apply my service brake.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 50 km/h when the oncoming passenger car suddenly appears. How will you continue?

answers.1: I pull over to the left side of the traffic lane.
answers.2: I brake hard.
answers.3: I brake gently.
answers.4: I hold back for the oncoming traffic.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 80 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 80 km/h.
answers.2: I reduce my speed.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the traffic signs.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road ahead.

questions.question_text: Which legal provisions apply to the use of the emergency telephone on a motorway?

answers.1: The emergency telephone is permitted to be used for particularly important messages to family members or to the workplace.
answers.2: The emergency telephone is permitted to be used only to call for assistance, for example in the event of a crash or similar incident.
answers.3: The emergency telephone is permitted to be used if my own mobile phone is not working.
answers.4: When I use the emergency telephone lawfully, I have a duty to stop the passenger car outside the traffic lane wherever possible.

questions.question_text: You have reduced your speed to approximately 30 km/h in order to turn right at the intersection. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the road condition in the traffic lane.
answers.2: I turn right at an unchanged speed of approximately 30 km/h.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the reduced traction.
answers.4: I reduce my speed further before turning right.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 40 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the pedestrian.
answers.2: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 40 km/h.
answers.3: I warn the pedestrian with a short horn signal.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 80 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 80 km/h.
answers.2: I reduce my speed.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of wild animals.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of horseback riders.

questions.question_text: You are driving on the right-hand side of a road with one-way traffic. What must you be particularly mindful of a bit further ahead?

answers.1: Oncoming traffic may appear.
answers.2: I hold back for the oncoming traffic.
answers.3: The oncoming traffic has a duty to hold back for me.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of the oncoming traffic.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 40 km/h and are required to pass the stationary vehicle. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 40 km/h.
answers.2: I drive past the stationary vehicle immediately.
answers.3: I apply my service brake.
answers.4: I hold back for the bicyclists.

questions.question_text: You are required to continue straight ahead on the road, which contains a bend. The two passenger cars are parked at the roadside. How will you continue?

answers.1: I drive past the two passenger cars immediately.
answers.2: I drive in between the two passenger cars and make space for the oncoming traffic.
answers.3: I stop before the two passenger cars and hold back for the oncoming traffic.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 50 km/h. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 50 km/h.
answers.2: I overtake the bicyclist.
answers.3: I reduce my speed.
answers.4: I remain behind the bicyclist for the time being.

questions.question_text: You have caught up with the two passenger cars with caravans that are driving slowly. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I overtake both caravans immediately.
answers.2: I overtake the first caravan and pull in in front of it.
answers.3: I remain behind both caravans and wait before overtaking.
answers.4: I pull over as far as possible to the right of the roadway.

questions.question_text: You are required to continue straight ahead through the intersection. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the pedestrian.
answers.2: I give a signal with the horn or a flashing light signal with my high-beam headlights.
answers.3: I increase my speed slightly.
answers.4: I brake and hold back for the pedestrian.

questions.question_text: You intend to turn right onto the side road. The lane on the right is clear. How do you proceed?

answers.1: I count on the bicyclist holding back for me.
answers.2: I turn right immediately.
answers.3: I hold back for the bicyclist.

questions.question_text: You intend to turn left here. There are no road users approaching from the right. How will you continue?

answers.1: I increase my speed.
answers.2: I turn left immediately.
answers.3: I hold back for the tractor approaching from the left.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 30 to 40 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead through the intersection. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of whether the grey passenger car is holding back for me.
answers.2: I continue across the intersection at an unchanged speed between 30 and 40 km/h.
answers.3: I move my foot over to the brake pedal.
answers.4: I am ready to hold back for vehicles approaching from the right side of the intersection.

questions.question_text: You intend to turn right at this intersection. The lane on the left is clear and there are no bicyclists behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the bicyclist approaching from the right.
answers.2: I warn the bicyclist with a short horn signal or a flashing light signal with my high-beam headlights.
answers.3: I turn right immediately.
answers.4: I hold back for the bicyclist approaching from the right.

questions.question_text: You are required to continue straight ahead through the intersection. There are no bicyclists behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I drive up to the stopline and stop.
answers.2: I drive forward to the passenger car at the intersection and wait behind it.
answers.3: I overtake the passenger car at the intersection on the right, going around it.

questions.question_text: According to the statutory requirements, what is the maximum permitted length of a vehicle with its load?

answers.1: A maximum length of 10 metres.
answers.2: A maximum length of 12 metres.
answers.3: A maximum length of 15 metres.

questions.question_text: You have stepped out of the passenger car here. According to the legal provisions on stopping and parking, which of the following applies here on a Saturday morning at 7 a.m.?

answers.1: I am permitted to park the passenger car here for one hour.
answers.2: I am permitted to stop the passenger car here for 2 to 3 minutes.

questions.question_text: The passenger car in front of you is driving so slowly that the truck overtakes it. How do you proceed?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road ahead.
answers.2: I give a flashing light signal with my high-beam headlights.
answers.3: I remain behind the passenger car.
answers.4: I follow the truck and overtake together with it.

questions.question_text: What does the hand signal given by the police officer mean for you?

answers.1: I am permitted to drive straight through the intersection.
answers.2: I have a duty to turn right at the intersection immediately.
answers.3: I have a duty to turn left at the intersection immediately.
answers.4: I have a duty to stop.

questions.question_text: You have stepped out of the passenger car here. According to the legal provisions, which of the following applies here?

answers.1: I am permitted to park the passenger car here.
answers.2: I am permitted to stop the passenger car here for 2 to 3 minutes.
answers.3: Stopping and parking are both prohibited here.
